prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG wuxia world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Hearts of Wulin / Jianghu inspired wuxia map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye Chinese ink-wash atlas view of a vast misty sea filled with many martial-world island groups, microcontinents, and hidden sect routes.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad misty ocean: several large mountain islands, microcontinents, tiny hermit islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, craggy islands, fragmented rocky coasts, temple islands, bamboo forest islands, pirate coves, hidden sect islands, sacred waterfall islands, desert islands, wetland islands, tundra highlands, river-market islands, and forbidden-manual sanctuaries.
Style/medium: Chinese ink wash continent map, jianghu locations in classical painting style, elegant hand-painted atlas, wuxia drama sourcebook cartography, subtle flowing chi energy trails as non-text decorative lines.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with misty sea border.
Lighting/mood: melodramatic wuxia epic, honor and betrayal, forbidden love, drifting mist, wandering heroes crossing seas and lakes, classical Chinese landscape beauty.
Color palette: ink black, soft mineral greens, mist grey, pale parchment, muted river blue, cinnabar red accents, light gold temple roofs.
Materials/textures: rice paper, brush ink coastlines, watercolor washes, calligraphic terrain strokes without readable characters, detailed waves and shoals.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable Chinese characters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
